subject: Ancient History
stem: Which one of the following ancient towns is well- known for its elaborate system of water harvesting and management by building a series of dams and channelizing water into connected reservoirs?

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: Dholavira
solution.detail: Indus Valley Civilization featured advanced town planning with well-laid-out streets, drainage systems, and standardized brick construction. Notable examples include the cities of Mohenjo-Daro, with its grid-like streets and Great Bath, and Harappa, known for its drainage systems and carefully planned residential areas. Option (a) is correct: Discovered in 1968 by Jagat Pati Joshi, Dholavira is one of the best-preserved Indus Valley sites, with a unique water management system using reservoirs and dams. Located between the Manhar and Mandsar streams, it was likely accessible by boat when the Rann’s water levels were higher.

stem: From the decline of Guptas until the rise of Harshavardhana in the early seventh century, which of the following kingdoms were holding power in Northern India? 1. The Guptas of Magadha 2. The Paramaras of Malwa 3. The Pushyabhutis of Thanesar 4. The Maukharis of Kanauj 5. The Yadavas of Devagiri 6. The Maitrakas of Valabhi Select the correct answer using the code given below:

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: 1, 3, 4 and 6
solution.detail: Post-Gupta India saw the rise of regional kingdoms and fragmented political power. This period marked a decline in centralized rule, leading to a more decentralized political landscape. Option (b) is correct: Due to Hun invasions, the Gupta kingdom was weakened in its power and several independent kingdoms arose in north India such as Yashodharman of Malwa, the Maukharis of Kanauj, the Maitrakas in Saurashtra and others. Pushyabhuti of Thanesar (Haryana) also carved an independent state around that time. After the coming of Tomars of Delhi in the 8th century, later by the 11-12th century, long after the decline of Gupta’s, Chauhans of Rajasthan, Solankis of Gujarat, Parmars of Malwa, and Chandels of Bundelkhand started ruling north India. It is only during the 14th CE, the Yadavas of Devagiri (Maharashtra) became an important ruling dynasty of Deccan.

stem: With reference to the history of ancient India, which of the following statements is/are correct? 1. Mitakshara was the civil law for upper castes and Dayabhaga was the civil law for lower castes. 2. In the Mitakshara system, the sons can claim right to the property during the lifetime of the father, whereas in the Dayabhaga system, it is only after the death of the father that the sons can claim right to the property. 3. The Mitakshara system deals with the matters related to the property held by male members only of a family, whereas the Dayabhaga system deals with the matters related to the property held by both male and female members of a family. Select the correct answer using the code given below:

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: 2 only
solution.detail: Statement 1 is incorrect: The distinction between Mitakshara and Dayabhaga is not based on caste i.e. upper or lower castes but on regional variations of Hindu law. Mitakshara was prevalent in most parts of India, whereas Dayabhaga was primarily followed in Bengal and parts of Assam. Statement 2 is correct: Both were Hindu law books and governed property rights and inheritance. The Mitakshara was written by Vijnanesvara, a scholar in the Kalyani Chalukya court in the late 11th century. The Dayabhaga was authored by Jimutavahana, a 12th-century scholar from Bengal. In the Mitakshara system, sons acquire a right to ancestral property by birth, meaning they have a share in the joint family property during the father’s lifetime. In the Dayabhaga system, sons do not have a right to the property until the father’s death. Statement 3 is incorrect: The Mitakshara system historically favored male descendants, with females becoming coparceners only after the Hindu Succession Act, 2005. In contrast, the Dayabhaga system provides relatively better property rights to women, allowing both male and female heirs to inherit property. However it is wrong to say that Mitakshara deals with matters of property rights of only males as the wife is allowed to have share when husband and son partition the property.

subject: Art and Culture
stem: With reference to Chausath Yogini Temple situated near Morena, consider the following statements: 1. It is a circular temple built during the reign of the Kachch- hapaghata Dynasty. 2. It is the only circular temple built in India. 3. It was meant to promote the Vaishnava cult in the region. 4. Its design has given rise to a popular belief that it was the inspiration behind the Indian Parliament building. Which of the statements given above are correct?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: 1 and 4
solution.detail: Statement 1 is correct: The Chausath Yogini temple is in Morena district in Madhya Pradesh. According to an inscription dated 1323 AD, this temple was built by Kachachapaghata king Devapala (1055-1075). Statement 2 is incorrect: The temple with a circular design pattern is situated on top of a small hill. It is not the only circular temple in India. Other examples are Kameshwar Temple, Kashmir and Vishnu Temple at Topra (Haryana) etc. Statement 3 is incorrect: Originally 64 yoginis were placed in 64 subsidiary shrines around the central shrine of Maha Shakti. Today Shiva linga have taken their place in shrines. The central shrine also holds a Shivalinga. Statement 4 is correct: This temple’s circular design has given rise to the popular idea that our old Indian parliament’s design designed by architects Edwin Lutyens and Herbert Baker was taken from here.

statements.text: The Chausath Yogini temple is in Morena district in Madhya Pradesh. According to an inscription dated 1323 AD, this temple was built by Kachachapaghata king Devapala (1055-1075).

The temple with a circular design pattern is situated on top of a small hill. It is not the only circular temple in India. Other examples are Kameshwar Temple, Kashmir and Vishnu Temple at Topra (Haryana) etc.
Originally 64 yoginis were placed in 64 subsidiary shrines around the central shrine of Maha Shakti. Today Shiva linga have taken their place in shrines. The central shrine also holds a Shivalinga.
This temple’s circular design has given rise to the popular idea that our old Indian parliament’s design designed by architects Edwin Lutyens and Herbert Baker was taken from here.

subject: Art and Culture
stem: With reference to India, the terms ‘Halbi, Ho and Kui’ pertain to:

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: Tribal languages
solution.detail:
India’s tribal languages reflect its rich cultural diversity, with hundreds of languages spoken by indigenous communities. These languages belong to various linguistic families, including Indo-Aryan and Dravidian. There are also other smaller language families, such as Austroasiatic (Ex-Munda languages) and Tibeto- Burman (Ex-Bodo). Examples of Tribal Languages include Halbi (Madhya Pradesh, Maharashtra), Ho (Jharkhand, Odisha), Kui (Odisha), Gondi (Central India), Korku (Madhya Pradesh), Kurukh (Odisha, Jharkhand), and Bhili (Rajasthan, Gujarat). Option (d) is correct:
- Halbi is spoken in Chhattisgarh, Madhya Pradesh, Odisha, and Maharashtra. It is an Indo-Aryan language influenced by Marathi and Odia. It is used by the Halba tribe and is recognized as a lingua franca among tribal communities in Bastar region.
- Ho is spoken in Jharkhand, Odisha, West Bengal, and Chhattisgarh. It belongs to the Munda language family which a branch of the Austroasiatic languages. The Ho tribe is an ethnic Munda community which primarily speaks this language. It has its own script called Warang Citi developed by tribal scholar Lako Bodra.
- Kui is a spoken mainly in Odisha by the Kandha (Kondh) tribe. It is a Dravidian language closely related to Gondi and Telugu. The Kandha tribe has a rich oral traditio and Kui plays a significant role in their cultural expressions.

subject: Economy
stem: Which one of the following is likely to be the most inflationary in its effects?
options: Borrowing from the public to finance a budget deficit
Borrowing from the banks to finance a budget deficit
Creation of new money to finance a budget deficit

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: Creation of new money to finance a budget deficit
solution.detail: Inflation is the general rise in prices of goods and services within an economy wherein, the purchasing power of consumers decreases, and the value of the cash holdings erode. The creation of new money to finance a Budget Deficit is highly inflationary. The government prints new money or borrows directly from the central bank (RBI in India) to fund its deficit. This directly increases the money supply in the economy without a corresponding increase in goods and services. It leads to excess liquidity in the economy causing demand-pull inflation.

subject: Economy
stem: Which of the following steps is most likely to be taken at the time of an economic recession?
options: Cut in tax rates accompanied by an increase in interest rate
Increase in expenditure on public projects
Increase in tax rates accompanied by reduction of interest rate
Reduction of expenditure on public projects

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: Increase in expenditure on public projects
solution.detail:
A fall in the gross domestic product (GDP) for two or more consecutive quarters is often regarded as an economic recession. Recessions are brought on by high interest rates because they reduce liquidity, or the quantity of money available for investment. Option (b) is correct: An increase in expenditure on public projects will lead to an increase in investments, leading to an increase in GDP and income in the economy and in turn increase in demand, completing the virtuous cycle of investment. Option (a), (c) and (d) is incorrect:
- Cutting taxes can increase disposable income, encouraging consumption and investment, which helps in a recession. However, raising interest rates makes borrowing more expensive, discouraging investment and consumption, which contradicts the objective of stimulating the economy during a recession.
- When income is falling in the economy then an increase in tax rates accompanied by a reduction of interest rate is not desirable at the moment.
- Reduction of expenditure on public projects in the time of recession will not be favourable as it will reduce the output of the economy.

optionNotes.text:
An increase in expenditure on public projects will lead to an increase in investments, leading to an increase in GDP and income in the economy and in turn increase in demand, completing the virtuous cycle of investment. Option (a), (c) and (d) is incorrect:
- Cutting taxes can increase disposable income, encouraging consumption and investment, which helps in a recession. However, raising interest rates makes borrowing more expensive, discouraging investment and consumption, which contradicts the objective of stimulating the economy during a recession.
- When income is falling in the economy then an increase in tax rates accompanied by a reduction of interest rate is not desirable at the moment.
- Reduction of expenditure on public projects in the time of recession will not be favourable as it will reduce the output of the economy.

solution.trick: If the private sector freezes, the public sector must step in. Hence option B aligns most. Option A and C are One foot on the brake, one on the accelerator” = bad policy mix, hence likely false. Option D, exactly the opposite of what’s needed, Govt pulling back = even less money in the economy, hence false.

subject: Economy
stem: With reference to the Indian economy, demand- pull inflation can be caused/increased by which of the following? 1. Expansionary policies 2. Fiscal stimulus 3. Inflation-indexing wages 4. Higher purchasing power 5. Rising interest rates Select the correct answer using the code given below.

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: 1, 2 and 4 only
solution.detail: Demand-pull inflation is caused by an increase in demand and wherein the demand in the economy outgrows the supply in the economy. It can be summed up as a condition of ‘too much money chasing too few goods’. With reference to the Indian economy, demand-pull inflation can be caused/increased by the following: 1. Expansionary policies: Money in the market rises when the government spends more freely. It leads to an increase in demand for the goods and fuels demand-pull inflation. 2. Fiscal Stimulus: Fiscal stimulus is a government-driven strategy that involves adjusting government spending and taxes to stimulate the economy. It also increases the money in the market which leads to an increase in demand for the goods and fuels demand-pull inflation 3. Higher Purchasing Power: Consumers feel more confident and spend more when they have a better income. As a result, demand increases, driving up inflation. Rising interest rates will reduce the money supply in the market. Borrowing money will become costlier, creating a credit crunch in the economy. So, it can not cause demand to pull inflation in the economy. Inflation-indexing wages means wages are linked to the inflation which means wages move as inflation changes in the economy. Such indexing is provided to reduce the effect of inflation on wages. It can not lead to demand pull inflation in the economy. Effective change in the wages is zero and it does not increase/ decrease purchasing power. So it cannot lead to demand-pull inflation in the economy.

subject: Economy
stem: Consider the following statements: Other things remaining unchanged, market demand for a good might increase if 1. 2. 3. price of its substitute increases price of its complement increases the good is an inferior good and the income of the consumer increases 4. its price falls Which of the above statements are correct?

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: 1 and 4 only
solution.detail: Statement 1 is correct: Substitutes are goods that can be used in place of each other (e.g., tea and coffee). If the price of a substitute increases, consumers will shift to the relatively cheaper good. If the price of a substitute good increases, consumers are more likely to switch to the cheaper substitute, thus increasing its demand. If the price of coffee rises, demand for tea may increase. Statement 2 is incorrect: Goods that are consumed together are called Complementary goods such as tea and sugar, pen and ink etc. When the price of a complement rises, the demand for the good usually decreases since the goods are consumed together. Since Pen and ink are used together, an increase in the price of pens is likely to reduce the demand for ink and a decrease in the price of ink is likely to increase the demand for pens. Statement 3 is incorrect: Inferior goods are those goods demanded for which move in the opposite direction as the income of the consumer. As the income of the customer increases, the demand for inferior goods falls, and as the income decreases, the demand for them rises. Consumers might switch from cheap local bread to premium brands as their income increases. Statement 4 is correct: According to the Law of Demand, if the price of a good falls, its quantity demanded increases, assuming other factors remain constant. subject: Economy
stem: Consider the following statements: 1. The Governor of the Reserve bank of India (RBI) is appointed by the Central Government. 2. Certain provisions in the Constitution of India give the Central Government the right to issue directions to the RBI in public interest. 3. The Governor of the RBI draws his power from the RBI Act. Which of the above statements are correct?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: 1 and 3 only
solution.detail: Statement 1 is correct: The Reserve Bank’’s affairs are governed by a central board of directors. The board is appointed by the Government of India in keeping with the Reserve Bank of India Act,1934. Statement 2 is incorrect: The Constitution of India does not contain provisions granting the Central Government the authority to issue directions to the RBI. However, such authority is provided under Section 7 of the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934. This section allows the Central Government to issue directions to the RBI in matters of public interest.Accordingly the Central Government may from time to time give such directions to the Bank as it may, after consultation with the Governor of the Bank, consider it necessary in the public interest. Statement 3 is correct: The powers and functions of the RBI Governor are derived from the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934. The Act outlines the roles, responsibilities, and authority of the Governor in managing the affairs of the RBI.

statements.text: The Reserve Bank’’s affairs are governed by a central board of directors. The board is appointed by the Government of India in keeping with the Reserve Bank of India Act,1934.

The Constitution of India does not contain provisions granting the Central Government the authority to issue directions to the RBI. However, such authority is provided under Section 7 of the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934. This section allows the Central Government to issue directions to the RBI in matters of public interest.Accordingly the Central Government may from time to time give such directions to the Bank as it may, after consultation with the Governor of the Bank, consider it necessary in the public interest.

The powers and functions of the RBI Governor are derived from the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934. The Act outlines the roles, responsibilities, and authority of the Governor in managing the affairs of the RBI.

subject: Economy
stem: With reference to ‘Urban Cooperative Banks’ in India consider the following statements: 1. They are supervised and regulated by local boards set up by the State Governments. 2. 3. They can issue equity shares and preference shares. They were brought under the purview of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 through an Amendment in 1966. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: 2 and 3 only
solution.detail:
A Co-operative bank belongs to its members, who are at the same time the owners and the customers of their bank. Co-operative banks are under dual control of the RBI and Registrar of Cooperative Societies. Agriculture, some small-scale businesses, and independent contractors primarily receive funding from cooperative banks. These banks are cooperative credit institutions that are registered under the Cooperative Societies Act 1912. These banks work according to the cooperative principles of mutual assistance. Co-operative banks have a three-tier structure:
- Primary Credit Societies-PCSs (agriculture or urban).
- District Central Co-Operative Banks- DCCBs.
- State Co-Operative Banks-SCBs (at the apex level). Statement 1 is incorrect: After Banking Regulation(Amendment) Act 2020 was passed, Most of the powers were transferred to RBI from the Registrars of the cooperative societies. Urban Cooperative Banks are subject to dual regulation by both the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) and the respective State Governments. The RBI oversees their banking operations, while the State Governments handle their incorporation, registration, management, and audit aspects through the Registrar of Cooperative Societies. Statement 2 is correct: RBI has issued guidelines to allow UCBs to raise capital by issuing equity shares and preference shares, similar to other banking institutions. This allows them to diversify their funding sources and strengthen their capital base. Statement 3 is correct: Urban Cooperative Banks (UCBs) were brought under the regulatory framework of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 through the Banking Laws (Application to Cooperative Societies) Act, 1965, which came into effect in 1966. This amendment extended the provisions of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949, to cooperative banks, including UCBs, bringing them under the supervision of the RBI.

subject: Economy
stem: In India, the central bank’s function as the ‘lender of last resort’ usually refers to which of the following? 1. Lending to trade and industry bodies when they fail to borrow from other sources 2. Providing liquidity to the banks having a temporary crisis 3. Lending to governments to finance budgetary deficits Select the correct answer using the code given below.

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: 2 only
solution.detail:
Statement 2 is correct: A “lender of last resort” means that the RBI (also called banker of banks) offers loans to banks or other eligible institutions that are experiencing financial difficulty or are considered highly risky or near collapse. By doing so, it helps stabilize the financial system and prevents bank runs and thereby safeguarding public confidence in the banking sector. Statements 1 and 3 are incorrect:
- Trade and industry bodies typically borrow from commercial banks or financial markets and not directly from the central bank. The RBI’s role as a lender of last resort is limited to the banking sector to ensure financial stability.
- Providing funds to cover government budgetary deficits is not part of the lender-of-last-resort function. The RBI’s support in such cases would fall under separate monetary management activities rather than emergency banking support.

statements.text:
A “lender of last resort” means that the RBI (also called banker of banks) offers loans to banks or other eligible institutions that are experiencing financial difficulty or are considered highly risky or near collapse. By doing so, it helps stabilize the financial system and prevents bank runs and thereby safeguarding public confidence in the banking sector. Statements 1 and 3 are incorrect:
- Trade and industry bodies typically borrow from commercial banks or financial markets and not directly from the central bank. The RBI’s role as a lender of last resort is limited to the banking sector to ensure financial stability.
- Providing funds to cover government budgetary deficits is not part of the lender-of-last-resort function. The RBI’s support in such cases would fall under separate monetary management activities rather than emergency banking support.

subject: Economy
stem: Which one of the following effects of the creation of black money in India has been the main cause of worry to the Government of India?
options: Diversion of resources to the purchase of real estate and investment in luxury housing
Investment in unproductive activities and purchase of precious stones, jewellery, gold, etc.
Large donations to political parties and growth of regionalism
Loss of revenue to the State Exchequer due to tax evasion

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: Loss of revenue to the State Exchequer due to tax evasion
solution.detail: Black money refers to income or wealth that is generated through illegal means or through legitimate activities that are not reported to the authorities for the purpose of avoiding taxes. It typically exists in the form of undeclared or unaccounted money, which is not taxed by the government. Option (a) is incorrect: While this is a consequence of black money, it is not the primary concern. The real estate market may become inflated due to investments made with black money, but this diversion of funds does not directly impact the government’s ability to generate revenue through taxation. However, it can lead to inefficient allocation of resources. Option (b) is incorrect: Black money often gets invested in unproductive assets like gold, precious stones, and luxury items. Although this can lead to economic inefficiencies, it does not directly cause a loss of government’s ability to generate revenue. The main problem lies in the undocumented nature of these transactions, leading to tax evasion. Option (c) is incorrect: Black money can be funneled into undocumented political donations and may contribute to regional political growth. However, the core issue remains the loss of tax revenue rather than the political consequences. Option (d) is correct: The main issue with black money is tax evasion, resulting in a significant loss of revenue for the government. Without the full collection of taxes, the government faces challenges in funding essential public services and development projects, which is the biggest concern for India’s economic health.

subject: Economy
stem: Consider the following statements: The effect of the devaluation of a currency is that it necessarily: 1. Improves the competitiveness of domestic exports in the foreign markets. 2. Increases the foreign value of the domestic currency 3. Improves the trade balance Which of the above statements is/are correct?

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: 1 only
solution.detail: Statement 1 is correct: Devaluation is a decrease in the value of a country’s currency relative to other currencies. It means it takes more of the domestic currency to buy one unit of a foreign currency. For example, if the exchange rate changes from ₹70 to ₹80 per dollar, the rupee has been devalued. Devaluation makes a country’s exports cheaper for foreign buyers. This can increase demand for exports, leading to higher export volumes and potentially improving the trade balance (exports minus imports). Statement 2 is incorrect: Devaluation means a decrease(not increase) in the value of the domestic currency relative to foreign currencies. It takes more of the domestic currency to buy one unit of a foreign currency. Statement 3 is incorrect: Devaluation can potentially improve the trade balance over time by boosting exports and reducing imports, but it is not guaranteed. The trade balance may deteriorate because import costs rise immediately, while export volumes may take time to increase. This phenomenon is called the J-curve effect. If a country devalues its currency, it still has to pay higher prices for imported oil, machinery, and technology. Over time, as foreign buyers demand more of its cheaper exports, the trade balance may improve.

solution.trick: "Necessarily" That’s a trap word — it implies guaranteed outcome. So we must be cautious, especially for S1 and 3. For S3, Devaluation can help improve trade balance (more exports, fewer imports), BUT: If your exports are not price-sensitive (inelastic) Or if import costs rise too much (like crude oil) It might not improve trade balance, or might even worsen it Because of “necessarily”, this statement fails the guarantee test. For S2, Devaluation = reducing the value of the domestic currency, hence likely false.

subject: Economy
stem: Consider the following: 1. Foreign currency convertible bonds 2. Foreign institutional investment with certain conditions 3. Global depository receipts 4. Non-resident external deposits Which of the above can be included in Foreign Direct Invest- ments?

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: 1, 2 and 3
solution.detail:
Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) refers to an investment made by an entity (typically a company or individual) from one country into a business or asset in another country, with the intent of establishing a lasting interest and exerting a degree of influence over the enterprise’s management and operations. It’s not just about a financial transaction but more about substantial and enduring involvement.
- Foreign currency convertible bonds (FCCBs) are debt instruments issued by a company in a foreign currency, which can be converted into equity shares at a future date. Because FCCBs can be converted into equity, and thus represent a potential ownership stake so They are considered a component of FDI.
- Foreign Institutional Investment (FII) are inves-tments made by foreign institutions in the financial markets of another country. In India, if an FII’s investment exceeds 10% of the post-issue paid-up equity capital of a company, it is reclassified as FD
- Global Depository Receipts (GDRs) are financial instruments used by companies to raise capital from international markets. They represent shares of a foreign company and are traded on international stock exchanges. Investments through GDRs are treated as FDI since they result in foreign equity participation in the issuing company.
- Non-Resident External (NRE) Deposits are deposits held by non-resident Indians (NRIs) in Indian banks in Indian rupees. They are primarily a means for NRIs to invest their savings in India. While they represent a flow of foreign funds into India, they are not considered FDI. They are more akin to portfolio investments or remittance as they don’t involve a direct stake in a business or substantial management influence.

subject: Economy
stem: Indian Government Bond Yields are influenced by which of the following? 1. Actions of the United States Federal Reserve 2. Actions of the Reserve bank of India 3. Inflation and short-term interest rates Select the correct answer using the code given below.

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: 1, 2 and 3
solution.detail: Bond yield is the return an investor gets on that bond or on a particular government security. It depends on the price of the bond which is impacted by its demand. Statement 1, 2 and 3 are correct: Factor Scenario Impact Effect on Bond Yields 1. US Federal Reserve Actions Raises Interest Rates Capital outflows from India as US bonds become more attractive. Yields rise due to lower demand for Indian bonds. Lowers Interest Rates Capital inflows to India as Indian bonds offer better returns. Yields fall due to higher demand for Indian bonds. 2. RBI Actions Increases Repo Rates (Tightening Policy) Higher borrowing costs slow down credit growth and economic activity. Yields rise as new bonds offer higher interest. Cuts Repo Rates (Easing Policy) Cheaper borrowing encourages economic activity and credit flow. Yields fall as new bonds are issued at lower rates. 3. Inflation and Short- Term Interest Rates Rising Inflation Reduces the real value of bond interest payments. Yields rise to compensate for inflation. Falling Inflation Improves real returns on bonds, making them more attractive. Yields fall due to increased bond demand.

stem: In nature, which of the following is/are most likely to be found surviving on a surface without soil? 1. Fern 2. Lichen 3. Moss 4. Mushroom Select the correct answer using the code given below:

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: 2 and 3
solution.detail:
Lichens and Mosses do not need soil to grow and survive.
- Lichens are generally found on rock, tree bark, soil (but can survive without soil as well), houses, tombstones, cars, old farm equipment, etc. Thus, lichen can be found surviving on surfaces without soil.
- Mosses are non-vascular plants. They do not need soil to survive.
- They have a rhizoid multi-cell anchoring structure that is used to climb and grip over rocky surfaces. Moss thrives in moist and shady areas. Ferns and Mushrooms generally need soil to grow.
- Ferns are vascular plants and typically require soil for anchorage and to absorb water and nutrients. While they can grow in places like cracks in rocks, they usually still need some form of substrate (soil or organic material) to thrive.
solution.trick: Observed Logic means using your real-life observations, everyday experiences, and visual memory to eliminate or select options — especially in questions related to environment, geography, culture, and biology. key principle: “If I’ve seen it in nature or in daily life, that’s evidence strong enough to trust in Prelims.” Now with this logic: You’ve likely seen moss on walls or stones, Needs moisture, not necessarily soil, Thrives on bare damp surfaces. hence 3 must be in correct option, eliminate options A and B.

subject: Environment

stem: Consider the following kinds of organisms: 1. Copepods 2. Cyanobacteria 3. Diatoms 4. Foraminifera Which of the above are primary producers in the food chains of oceans?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: 2 and 3
solution.detail:
Primary Producers are organisms like plants and algae that produce their own food through photosynthesis or chemosynthesis. They form the base of the food chain in marine ecosystems. Cyanobacteria and Diatoms are primary producers.
- Cyanobacteria (also known as blue-green algae) are photosynthetic bacteria that play a significant role in marine ecosystems by converting sunlight into energy and producing oxygen. They are particularly important in nutrient-poor (oligotrophic) oceanic regions.
- Diatoms are a type of phytoplankton that perform photosynthesis. They are among the most important primary producers in the oceans which contribute significantly to global carbon fixation. They are a key food source for zooplankton and other marine organisms. Copepods and Foraminifera are consumers.
- Copepods are small crustaceans that are not primary producers. They are zooplankton that feed on phytoplankton (primary producers) and other small organisms. They play a crucial role in marine food webs as a link between primary producers and higher trophic levels.
- Foraminifera are not primary producers. They are singlecelled protists that belong to the zooplankton group. They feed on phytoplankton, detritus, and other small organisms. They are important in marine ecosystems as indicators of environmental conditions.

stem: Which of the following are detritivores? 1. Earthworms 2. Jellyfish 3. Millipedes 4. Seahorses 5. Woodlice Select the correct answer using the code given below

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: 1, 3 and 5 only
solution.detail: Detritivores also known as detritivores, detritophages, detritus feeders, or detritus eaters are heterotrophs that obtain nutrients by consuming detritus (decomposing plant and animal parts as well as feces). By doing so, all these detritivores contribute to decomposition and the nutrient cycles. Option (c) is correct: Earthworms, Millipedes and Woodlice are Detritivores. Detritivores are an important aspect of many ecosystems. They can live on any type of soil with an organic component, including marine ecosystems, where they are termed interchangeably with bottom feeders. Typical detritivorous animals include earthworm, millipedes, springtails, woodlice, dung flies, slugs, many terrestrial worms, sea stars, sea cucumbers, fiddler crabs, and some sedentary polychaetes such as worms of the family Terebellidae.

subject: Environment

stem: In which one of the following biogeochemical cycles, the weathering of rocks is the main source of release of nutrients to enter the cycle?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Phosphorus cycle
solution.detail: The phosphorus cycle is unique for not have a significant atmospheric component. Phosphorus is primarily released into the cycle through the weathering of rocks containing phosphate minerals (e.g., apatite). Weathering releases phosphate ions (PO₄³⁻) into soil and water, making it available for uptake by plants and other organisms. Phosphorus is one of the most important nutrients in biological systems since it is a fundamental ingredient of nucleic acids, phospholipids, and several phosphorylated compounds.

stem: Which of the following have species that can establish a symbiotic relationship with other organisms? 1. Cnidarians 2. Fungi 3. Protozoa Select the correct answer using the code given below

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: 1, 2 and 3
solution.detail:
All of the given species can establish a symbiotic relationship with other organisms. Symbiotic Relationship:
- A relationship between two (or more) species that may or may not be advantageous to one or both is referred to as symbiotic.
- Symbiosis typically occurs between different species. Interactions within the same species (intraspecific) are usually not categorized as symbiosis but may involve other forms of cooperation. For eg., Coral has a symbiotic relationship (each gives something to the other and gets something back in return) with ‘zooxanthellae’ microscopic algae which live on coral. Option 1 is correct: The relationship between cnidarians and dinoflagellate algae is termed “symbiotic” because both the animal host and the algae are benefiting from the association. It is a mutualistic interaction. Option 2 is correct: Two common mutualistic relationships involving fungi are mycorrhiza (fungi and plant roots) and lichen (fungi and either cyanobacteria or green algae). Option 3 is correct: Protozoa mostly represent a close mutualistic association between a protozoan and unicellular symbionts such as bacteria, cyanobacteria or/and unicellular algae or protozoans and a multicellular organisms such as ruminants, lower termites, wood-eating cockroaches, plants.
solution.trick: There are so many species in each, that saying NOT even one of them can establish a symbiotic relationship would be highly improbable, hence all are likely true.

subject: Environment
stem: Which one of the following is used in preparing a natural mosquito repellent?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Lemongrass
solution.detail: Lemongrass is a tall herb that is widely distributed in tropical and subtropical regions of Asia, Africa, and Australia. Lemongrass is most frequently produced in China and India. The lemongrass herb is well known for its effectiveness at warding off mosquitoes and works as a mosquito repellent. It contains the chemical citronella, which aids in disguising the smells that mosquitoes use to target victims. A 2011 study found that the strong odour of lemongrass oil effectively killed or fully repelled more than 90% of the mosquitoes from a particular range of species.

subject: Environment
stem: The vegetation of savannah consists of grassland with scattered small trees, but extensive areas have no trees. The forest development in such areas is generally kept in check by one or more or a combination of some conditions. Which of the following are such conditions? 1. Burrowing animals and termites 2. Fire 3. Grazing herbivores 4. Seasonal rainfall 5. Soil properties Select the correct answer using the code given below.

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: 2, 3 and 4
solution.detail:
Savanna Biome is a particular kind of grassland biome made up of vast expanses of open grassland with few trees. Tropical and Semi-tropical savannas are two kinds of savannas. All continents excluding Antarctica have grasslands. The largest savannas are found close to the equator in Africa. Option (c) is correct:
- Frequent wildfires both natural (lightning-induced) and human-induced prevent the establishment of dense forests. Fires destroy young tree saplings before they can mature into large trees, thus maintaining grasslands. Savanna fires are a key ecological process that helps maintain the balance between grasslands and scattered trees.
- Large herbivores like elephants, zebras, and antelopes feed on young tree shoots, preventing forests from expanding. Grazing by animals ensures that tree cover remains sparse which allows grasses to dominate. Studies on African and Indian savannas have shown that herbivory is a critical limiting factor for tree growth.
- The limited and unpredictable rainfall pattern allows grasses to outcompete trees for available water. Savannas experience distinct wet and dry seasons leading to limited water availability. Trees require consistent moisture to grow into dense forests but in savannas prolonged dry periods hinder tree survival. Burrowing animals such as aardvarks and rodents and termites play a role in soil aeration and nutrient cycling. But they do not significantly prevent tree growth or forest development. Trees can grow in varied soil types and soil properties alone do not explain forest suppression.

subject: Environment
stem: How is permaculture farming different from conventional chemical farming? 1. Permaculture farming discourages monocultural practices but in conventional chemical farming, monoculture practices are predominant. 2. Conventional chemical farming can cause an increase in soil salinity but the occurrence of such a phenomenon is not observed in permaculture farming. 3. Conventional chemical farming is easily possible in semi-arid regions but permaculture farming is not so easily possible in such regions. 4. Practice of mulching is very important in permaculture farming but not necessarily so in conventional chemical farming. Select the correct answer using the code given below

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: 1, 2 and 4
solution.detail: Permaculture is a totally integrated design system that’s modelled on nature. Statement 1 is correct: Permaculture farming promotes multi-cropping and integrated farming systems. While chemical farming is more suitable for monoculture cropping due to the use of crop-specific inputs like irrigation, chemical fertiliser and harvesting methods. Statement 2 is correct: Conventional farming usually alters the natural environment, increases soil salinity, and eliminates biodiversity. Such problems are not seen in permaculture farming as it relies on organic fertilizers. Statement 3 is incorrect: Chemical farming is based on the intense use of inputs like chemical fertilizers and irrigation that make the soil less fertile over time, thus restricting its practice in semi-arid regions. Thus, conventional farming is not suitable for semi-arid regions. Permaculture involves well-designed systems that don’t produce waste and permaculture tries to imitate well-designed systems. Thus, it can work in arid climates as well. Statement 4 is correct: Mulching is an important component of Permaculture systems for promoting maximum efficiency.

subject: Environment
stem: Why is there a concern about copper smelting plants? 1. They may release lethal quantities of carbon monoxide into the environment. 2. The copper slag can cause the leaching of some heavy metals into the environment. 3. They may release sulphur dioxide as a pollutant. Select the correct answer using the code given below.

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: 2 and 3 only
solution.detail:
Copper Smelting is a process of applying heat to ore in order to extract a base metal. Smelting uses heat and a chemical reducing agent to decompose the ore, driving off other elements as gases or slag and leaving the metal base behind. It is the method of extracting pure elemental copper (Cu) from copper concentrate (CuFeS2). Statement 1 is incorrect: Copper smelting generally does not release lethal quantities of carbon monoxide (CO) into the environment. Statement 2 and 3 are correct: Some smelting processes can also produce large quantities of solid waste, known as slag. This slag may leach heavy metals (arsenic, cadmium, lead or mercury depending on the composition of the ore) into groundwater reservoirs. Smelting processes are known to emit high quantities of air pollutants such as hydrogen fluoride, sulphur dioxide, oxides of nitrogen, offensive and noxious smoke fumes, vapours, gases, and other toxins.
- Its major advantages are:  High energy content, making it an efficient fuel choice for heating purposes  Often cost-competitive compared to other heating options  Readily available in many regions, ensuring a consistent supply
- Furnace/fuel oil combustion can produce emissions, including sulfur dioxide (SO2), nitrogen oxides (NOx), and particulate matter

Copper smelting generally does not release lethal quantities of carbon monoxide (CO) into the environment. Statement 2 and 3 are correct: Some smelting processes can also produce large quantities of solid waste, known as slag. This slag may leach heavy metals (arsenic, cadmium, lead or mercury depending on the composition of the ore) into groundwater reservoirs. Smelting processes are known to emit high quantities of air pollutants such as hydrogen fluoride, sulphur dioxide, oxides of nitrogen, offensive and noxious smoke fumes, vapours, gases, and other toxins.

- Furnace/fuel oil combustion can produce emissions, including sulfur dioxide (SO2), nitrogen oxides (NOx), and particulate matter

subject: Environment
stem: With reference to furnace oil, consider the following statements: 1. It is a product of oil refineries. 2. Some industries use it to generate power. 3. Its use causes sulphur emissions into the environment. Which of the statements given above are correct?

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: 1, 2 and 3
solution.detail: Statement 1 is correct: Furnace oil or fuel oil is a dark viscous residual product of crude-oil distillation. It is used as a fuel in different types of combustion equipment. Statement 2 is correct: Marine engines and slow speed engines use Furnace oil for power generation, they are used for drying tea leaves. Thermic fluid heaters and hot air generators also use Furnace oil. Statement 3 is correct: The emissions of oxides of sulphur are a direct result of the sulphur content of the fuel oil.

subject: Environment
stem: Magnetite particles, suspected to cause neurodeg- enerative problems, are generated as environmental pollutants from which of the following? 1. Brakes of motor vehicles 2. Engines of motor vehicles 3. Microwave stoves within homes 4. Power plants 5. Telephone lines Select the correct answer using the code given below.

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: 1, 2 and 4 only
solution.detail: Magnetite particles are a highly magnetic mineral form of iron and are known to be present in air pollution. Exposure to airborne particulate matter (PM) is associated with pulmonary, cardiovascular and neurological problems. Option 1 is correct: Vehicle brake systems are the major source of airborne magnetite at the roadside. Option 2 is correct: Petrol and diesel-engine exhaust are also a source of airborne magnetite. Option 4 is correct: Other sources of Magnetite pollution include Power plants. Options 3 and 5 are incorrect: Household microwave stoves and telephone lines not are established as sources of airborne magnetite particles. Other sources of Magnetite pollution include microwave Stoves within homes, As per WHO, Telephone lines produce low frequency, low energy electromagnetic fields which are the potential source of magnetite particles.

subject: Environment
stem: The ‘Common Carbon Metric’, supported by UNEP, has been developed for
options: Assessing the carbon footprint of building operations around the world
Enabling commercial farming entities around the world to enter carbon emission trading
Enabling governments to assess the overall carbon footprint caused by their countries
Assessing the overall carbon foot-print caused by the use of fossil fuels by the world in a unit time Environment and Ecology 487

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: Assessing the carbon footprint of building operations around the world
solution.detail: The Common Carbon Metric’ is a protocol developed by United Nations Environment Program’s Sustainable Buildings & Climate Initiative (UNEP-SBCI) for measuring energy use & reporting GHG emissions from Building Operations. Its purpose is to support greenhouse gas (GHG) emission’s reductions through accurate measurement of energy efficiency improvements in building operations.

subject: Environment
stem: What is blue carbon?
options: Carbon captured by oceans and coastal ecosystems
Carbon sequestered in forest biomass and agricultural soils
Carbon contained in petroleum and natural gas.

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: Carbon captured by oceans and coastal ecosystems
solution.detail: Blue Carbon is the carbon captured by the world’s ocean and coastal ecosystems. There are three types of coastal ecosystems — mangroves, seagrasses and tidal marshes; these store half the “blue” carbon buried beneath the ocean floor. These areas absorb and store carbon at a much faster rate than other areas, such as forests, and can continue to do so for millions of years. The carbon found in coastal soil is often thousands of years old.

subject: Environment
stem: With reference to the ‘New York Declaration on Forests’, which of the following statements are correct? 1. It was first endorsed at the United Nations Climate Summit in 2014. 2. It endorses a global timeline to end the loss of forests. 3. It is a legally binding international declaration. 4. It is endorsed by governments, big companies and indigenous communities. 5. India was one of the signatories at its inception. Select the correct answer using the code given below.

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: 1, 2 and 4
solution.detail: Statement 1 is correct: The NYDF was launched and endorsed at the United Nations Climate Summit in New York on September 23, 2014. It was a significant global effort to address deforestation and promote forest conservation. Statement 2 is correct: The NYDF sets a global timeline to end natural forest loss by 2030 and to restore 350 million hectares of degraded forest and agricultural land by the same year. It also aims to halve deforestation by 2020 (though this target was not met). Statement 3 is incorrect: The New York Declaration on Forests (NYDF) is a voluntary and non-legally binding international declaration aimed at ending global deforestation. Statement 4 is correct: The NYDF has been endorsed by a wide range of stakeholders, including national governments, multinational companies, NGOs, and indigenous communities. Over 200 entities have pledged their support. Statement 5 is incorrect: India was not a signatory to the NYDF at its inception. While many countries, including the United States, Canada, and several European nations, endorsed the declaration, India has still not joined as a signatory.

Legally binding is strong requirement, seldom international declaration are legally binding, hence more likely false. Eliminating options B and C. Now as compare to S5(much more specific and hence easy to manipulate) S2 is much more general and practical to be false.

subject: Environment
stem: Consider the following animals: 1. Hedgehog 2. Marmot 3. Pangolin To reduce the chance of being captured by predators, which of the above organisms rolls up/roll up and protects/protects its/ their vulnerable parts?

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: 1 and 3
solution.detail: Option 1 is correct: Hedgehogs are small mammals with short limbs and a body low to the ground. Their most distinctive characteristic is the thousands of stiff, sharp spines - harder and sharper than those of a porcupine - that cover the animal’s back and sides, like a pincushion filled with needles. If attacked they will curl into a prickly and unappetizing ball that deters most predators. Option 2 is incorrect: Marmot are any of 14 species of giant ground squirrels found primarily in North America and Eurasia. Marmots are well suited for life in cold environments and have small fur-covered ears, short, stocky legs, and strong claws for digging. Marmots do not roll up and protect their vulnerable parts. They often use their sharp teeth and powerful jaws to defend themselves. Option 3 is correct: Pangolin, also called scaly anteaters because of their preferred diet. They are the most trafficked mammal in the world. If touched or grabbed it will roll up completely into a ball, while the sharp scales on the tail can be used to lash out.

subject: Environment
stem: Which one of the following is a filter feeder?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Oyster
solution.detail:
Option (c) is correct: Filter feeders are a subgroup of suspension feeding animals that feed by straining suspended matter and food particles from water, typically bypassing the water over a specialised filtering structure. Clams, krill, sponges, oysters, etc are among the creatures that rely on this way of feeding (including some sharks). Filter feeders include some birds like flamingos and several varieties of duck. Filter feeders are regarded as ecosystem engineers since they might be crucial in the clarification of water. They serve as indicator organisms and are crucial in bioaccumulation. Option (a), (b) and (d) are incorrect:
- Catfish are bottom-dwellers that feed on a variety of food sources, including insects, crustaceans, and small fish.
- Octopuses are predators that feed on crustaceans, mollusks, and fish.
- Pelicans are birds that feed on fish and other aquatic animals.

Palm oil is a raw material for some industries producing lipstick and perfumes. 3. The palm oil can be used to produce biodiesel. Which of the statements given above are correct?

subject: Environment
stem: Consider the following statements: 1. 2. 3. Moringa (drumstick tree) is a leguminous evergreen tree. The Tamarind tree is endemic to South Asia. In India, most of the tamarind is collected as minor forest produce. 4. 5. India exports tamarind and seeds of moringa. Seeds of moringa and tamarind can be used in the production of biofuels. Which of the statements given above are correct?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: 3, 4 and 5
solution.detail: Leguminous plants have small pockets or nodules in their roots that host a specific type of bacteria known as nitrogen-fixing bacteria. Statements 1 and 2 are incorrect: Moringa is a rapidly growing, drought-tolerant tree native to the Indian subcontinent. It is found naturally in the sub-Himalayan regions of Northern India and is now cultivated globally in tropical and subtropical climates. Unlike leguminous plants, Moringa is not a legume and is deciduous, shedding its leaves seasonally. On the other hand, tamarind, a leguminous tree known for its edible fruit, is native to tropical Africa, not South Asia. Statements 3 and 4 are correct: In India most of the Tamarind is collected as Minor Forest Produce with Minimum Support Price. Tamarind is also actively exported from India. Statement 5 is correct: Biodiesel is derived from the tamarind seed through the transesterification process as potential alternative feedstock for the diesel engine. Seeds of Moringa too can be used for biodiesel production.

subject: Environment
stem: 'R2 Code of Practices' constitutes a tool available for promoting the adoption of:
options: environmentally responsible practices in electronics recycling industry
ecological management of Wetlands of International Importance under the Ramsar Convention
sustainable practices in the cultivation of agricultural crops in degraded lands
‘Environmental Impact Assessment’ in the exploitation of natural resources

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: environmentally responsible practices in electronics recycling industry
solution.detail:
The R2 Code of Practices (R2 Standard) is a set of guidelines designed to promote environmentally responsible practices in the electronics recycling industry. It provides a framework for recyclers to ensure that electronic waste (e-waste) is managed in a way that protects human health, safety, and the environment. The R2 Standard covers areas such as:
- Safe handling and disposal of hazardous materials.
- Data security and destruction.
- Worker health and safety.
- Environmental responsibility.

subject: Geography
stem: Consider the following statements: 1. In the tropical zone, the western sections of the oceans are warmer than the eastern sections owing to the influence of trade winds. 2. In the temperate zone, westerlies make the eastern sections of oceans warmer than the western sections. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Both 1 and 2
solution.detail: Statement 1 is correct: In the tropical zone, trade winds blow from the east towards the west. These winds push warm surface water towards the western parts of the oceans (e.g., the western Pacific Ocean and the western Atlantic Ocean). As a result, the water in the western parts of tropical oceans is warmer than in the eastern parts. The eastern sections of the oceans tend to have upwelling, where colder, nutrient-rich waters from deeper layers rise to the surface. Statement 2 is correct: In the temperate zone, the westerlies (winds blowing from the west towards the east) push the surface water eastward, causing the western parts of oceans (like the western coast of continents) to be colder. The water in the eastern sections of oceans tends to be warmer due to the Ekman transport, which moves warm water towards the eastern coasts. For example, in the North Atlantic Ocean, the Gulf Stream carries warm water from the tropics to the eastern coasts of North America and Europe, keeping the eastern sections warmer than the western parts.

The eastern sections of the oceans tend to have upwelling, where colder, nutrient-rich waters from deeper layers rise to the surface.

subject: Geography
stem: “Leaf litter decomposes faster than in any other biome and as a result the soil surface is often almost bare. Apart from trees, the vegetation is largely composed of plant forms that reach up into the canopy vicariously, by climbing the trees or growing as epiphytes, rooted on the upper branches of trees.” This is the most likely description of :

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: Tropical rainforest
solution.detail: Option (d) is correct: The description refers to the Tropical Rainforest or Hot, Wet Equatorial Climate. In this biome, leaf litter decomposes rapidly due to high humidity and temperatures, quickly returning nutrients to the soil. As a result, the soil surface often appears bare with minimal leaf litter. They are home to climbing plants (lianas) and epiphytes (plants that grow on trees), which reach the canopy to access sunlight. These unique plant forms and rapid decomposition are characteristic features of tropical rainforests, making it a highly dynamic and nutrient-efficient ecosystem. Options (a), (b) and (c) are incorrect: Coniferous forests (or taiga) are characterized by cold climates and slow decomposition rates. The leaf litter in coniferous forests decomposes more slowly than in tropical rainforests due to the lower temperatures and acidic nature of the soil. The description of rapid decomposition and bare soil does not match the characteristics of coniferous forests. Dry deciduous forests experience dry seasons where many trees shed their leaves. While leaf litter does decompose, the process is slower due to the dry conditions. These forests do not have the constant, year-round decomposition found in tropical rainforests. Additionally, the mention of epiphytes and climbing plants is less common in dry deciduous forests. Mangrove forests are coastal ecosystems, typically found in tropical and subtropical regions. They are characterized by salt-tolerant plants and the intertidal zone, not by the rapid decomposition of leaf litter. The soil in mangrove forests is often muddy, and the vegetation is adapted to waterlogged conditions rather than the “bare” soil and climbing plants described in the given statement.

subject: Geography
stem: The black cotton soil of India has been formed due to the weathering of:

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: Fissure volcanic rock
solution.detail: Black cotton soil, also known as regur soil, is highly valued in India because of its excellent moisture-retaining properties and suitability for cotton cultivation. Black cotton soil is primarily formed from the weathering of basaltic rocks, which are a type of fissure volcanic rock. These basaltic rocks were originally erupted during the Cretaceous period as part of the Deccan Traps in the northwest Deccan plateau. The weathering process of these volcanic rocks releases minerals that, combined with climatic factors, result in the formation of the distinctive black soil. The high clay content of black cotton soil enables it to retain moisture efficiently. Its properties make it particularly suitable for cotton cultivation, hence the alternative name “black cotton soil.”

subject: Geography
stem: Among the following, which one is the least water- efficient crop ?

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: Sugarcane
solution.detail: Option (a) is correct: Sugarcane is the least water-efficient crop. It requires around 1800 to 2200 mm of water, making it a water-intensive crop. It has high water requirements, primarily because it is grown in tropical climates where irrigation is essential for optimal growth. Option (b) is incorrect: Major oilseeds like soyabean, castor seeds, cotton seeds, linseed and sunflower, etc. requires around 672.4 mm of water, which is considerably lower than sugarcane’s water needs, making it more water-efficient. Option (c) is incorrect: Millets can be grown even in dry regions without irrigation facilities. Pearl millet is a drought-resistant crop that requires only 350 mm of water, making it highly water-efficient, especially in dry regions.

subject: Geography
stem: With reference to the Indus river system, of the following four rivers, three of them pour into one of them which joins the Indus directly. Among the following, which one is such a river that joins the Indus directly ?

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: Sutlej
solution.detail: Option (a) is incorrect: Chenab River originates from Bara Lacha Pass in Himachal Pradesh. It is formed by the confluence of Chandra and Bhaga rivers in Himachal Pradesh, flows through Jammu and Kashmir and enters Pakistan. The Chenab does not join the Indus directly. It merges with the Sutlej River in Pakistan. Option (b) is incorrect: Jhelum River originates from Verinag Spring in Jammu and Kashmir. It flows through the Kashmir Valley and enters Pakistan. Jhelum joins Chenab, which later merges with Sutlej before reaching the Indus. Jhelum does not directly join Indus. Option (c) is incorrect: Ravi River originates from Bara Bhangal in Himachal Pradesh. It flows through Punjab before entering Pakistan. Ravi joins the Chenab River, which later merges with the Sutlej before meeting the Indus. Option (d) is correct: Sutlej River originates from Rakshastal, near Lake Mansarovar, Tibet. It flows through Himachal Pradesh and Punjab before entering Pakistan. Sutlej is the only river that directly joins the Indus River at Mithankot, Pakistan. Thus, it is the only Satluj river in the Indus valley system which receives water from three rivers of the system and joins Indus directly. The Indus River System: Fig: Map of the Indus River System

subject: Geography
stem: With reference to India, Didwana, Kuchaman, Sargol and Khatu are the names of

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: saline lakes
solution.detail: A salt lake or saline lake is a landlocked body of water that has a concentration of salts and other dissolved minerals significantly higher than most lakes. These lakes are characterized by their high salt content, often due to the arid or semi-arid climate and the lack of outflowing rivers leading to the evaporation of water and the concentration of salts. Didwana, Kuchaman, Sargol and Khatu are saline lakes of Rajasthan.

subject: Geography
stem: Consider the following rivers : 1. Brahmani 2. Nagavali 3. Subarnarekha 4. Vamsadhara Which of the above rise from the Eastern Ghats ?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: 2 and 4
solution.detail: Option 1 is incorrect: The Brahmani River does not originate in the Eastern Ghats. The Brahmani River is formed by the confluence of the South Koel and Sankh rivers in Jharkhand. The South Koel originates near Nagri in Jharkhand, while the Sankh River originates in the Chotanagpur Plateau. After the confluence, it flows through the Eastern Ghats in Sundargarh, Deogarh, Dhenkanal, and Jajpur districts. Option 2 is correct: The Nagavali River originates in the Eastern Ghats near Lakhbahal in the Kalahandi district of Odisha. It flows through Odisha and Andhra Pradesh before draining into the Bay of Bengal. Option 3 is incorrect: The Subarnarekha River originates near Nagri in the Chotanagpur Plateau of Jharkhand. It flows through Jharkhand, West Bengal, and Odisha before emptying into the Bay of Bengal. The Chotanagpur Plateau is part of the Deccan Plateau, not the Eastern Ghats. Option 4 is correct: The Vamsadhara River rises in the Durgakangar Hills of the Eastern Ghats in the Kalahandi district of Odisha. It flows through Odisha and Andhra Pradesh before draining into the Bay of Bengal.

subject: International Relations
stem: Consider the following statements: 1. The Global Ocean Commission grants licences for seabed ex- ploration and mining in international waters. 2. India has received licences for seabed mineral exploration in international waters. 3. ‘Rare earth minerals’ are present on the seafloor in interna- tional waters. Which of the statements given above are correct?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: 2 and 3 only
solution.detail: Statement 1 is incorrect: The Global Ocean Commission (GOC) does not grant licenses for seabed mining. The International Seabed Authority (ISA) established under the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS) is responsible for granting licenses for seabed exploration and mining in international waters. Statement 2 is correct: India has been granted licenses by the International Seabed Authority (ISA) for seabed mineral exploration in international waters. India has received a contract to explore polymetallic sulfides in the Indian Ocean Ridge. The Indian Ministry of Earth Sciences and NIOT (National Institute of Ocean Technology) are leading the project. Statement 3 is correct: Rare earth elements (REEs) are found in deep-sea sediments, polymetallic nodules, and hydrothermal vents in international waters. These include elements like neodymium, yttrium, and dysprosium, which are essential for electronics, renewable energy, and defense industries.

The International Seabed Authority (ISA) established under the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS) is responsible for granting licenses for seabed exploration and mining in international waters.

subject: International Relations
stem: Consider the following statements in respect of the ICC World Test Championship: 1. The finalists were decided by the number of matches they won. 2. New Zealand was ranked ahead of England because it won more matches than England. Which of the above statements is/are correct?

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: Neither 1 nor 2
solution.detail: Statement 1 is incorrect: Instead, the ranking was based on the percentage of points (PCT) earned from matches played. Due to COVID-19 disruptions, the ICC modified the ranking system in 2020, and PCT (Points Percentage System) became the deciding factor rather than just the number of wins. Statement 2 is incorrect: England won more matches than New Zealand in the WTC cycle (England won 11, New Zealand won 7). However, New Zealand had a higher PCT (70%) compared to England (61.4%), securing their place in the final.

subject: International Relations
stem: Consider the following statements: Statement 1: The United Nations Capital Development Fund (UNCDF) and the Arbor Day Foundation have recently recog- nized Hyderabad as 2020 Tree City of the World. Statement 2: Hyderabad was selected for the recognition for a year following its commitment to grow and maintain the urban forests. Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?
options: Both Statement 1 and Statement 2 are correct and Statement 2 is the correct explanation for Statement 1.
Both Statement 1 and Statement 2 are correct but Statement 2 is not the correct explanation for Statement 1.
Statement 1 is correct but Statement 2 is not correct.
Statement 1 is not correct but Statement 2 is correct.

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: Statement 1 is not correct but Statement 2 is correct.

stem: Consider the following statements : 1. 21st February is declared to be the International Mother Lan- guage Day by UNICEF. 2. The demand that Bangla has to be one of the national languag- es was raised in the Constituent Assembly of Pakistan. Which of the above statements is/are correct?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: 2 only
solution.detail: Statement 1 is incorrect: 21st February is recognized as International Mother Language Day by UNESCO, not UNICEF. It was proclaimed by UNESCO in 1999 and observed worldwide since 2000. The day commemorates the 1952 Bengali Language Movement in East Pakistan (now Bangladesh), where protesters were killed for demanding Bangla as an official language. Statement 2 is correct: In 1948, Dhirendranath Datta, a member from East Pakistan (now Bangladesh), raised the demand for Bangla to be recognized as one of the national languages of Pakistan in the Constituent Assembly of Pakistan. The West Pakistani leadership rejected the demand, leading to protests and eventually the Bengali Language Movement (Bhasha Andolon). This movement later contributed to the liberation of Bangladesh in 1971.

options: 1, 2 and 3 International Relations and Current Affairs 673

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: 1, 2 and 3 International Relations and Current Affairs 673

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: 1 only
solution.detail: Statement 1 is correct: In the pursuit of the fugitive Khwarezm prince, Changez Khan arrived in India during the reign of Iltutmish. By denying shelter to Prince Jalaluddhin of Central Asia, Iltutmish protected India from Changez Khan’s fury. Statement 2 is incorrect: The period of Muhammad bin Tughlaq was from 1325 to 1351. Taimur invaded India during the reign of Nasir-ud-Din Mahmud Shah Tughluq (1394-1412), the last Tughlaq ruler. At the time of his arrival, the ruler of the Vijayanagara Empire was Krishnadevaraya (reigned 1509– 1529).

subject: Medieval History
stem: With reference to Indian history, which of the following statements is/are correct? 1. The Nizamat of Arcot emerged from Hyderabad State. 2. The Mysore Kingdom emerged from the Vijayanagara Empire. 3. Rohilkhand Kingdom was formed out of the territories occupied by Ahmad Shah Durrani. Select the correct answer using the code given below:

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: 2 only
solution.detail: Statement 1 is incorrect: The Nizamat of Arcot was a Muslim princely state in South India during the 18th century. It was founded by the Nawabs of Arcot, who initially served as vassals under the Nizam, the ruler of the Hyderabad State. After the weakening of the Nizam’s power, they gained greater control over the region. The Nizamat of Arcot included areas such as Arcot, Carnatic, and parts of modern-day Tamil Nadu. Statement 2 is correct: Under the Vijayanagar Empire, there was a small feudal kingdom called Mysore. The Kingdom of Mysore was founded by the Wodeyar dynasty in 1399. The Wodeyar dynasty declared their independence following the fall of Vijayanagar in 1565, and Raja Wodeyar ascended the throne in 1578. The capital moved from Mysore to Srirangapatna in 1610. Statement 3 is incorrect: Afghan Chiefs under the leadership of Daud Khan established Rohilakhand, from erstwhile Mughal territory in Awadh region, by taking advantage of the breakdown of authority in North India following Nadir Shah’s invasion.

statements.text: The Nizamat of Arcot was a Muslim princely state in South India during the 18th century. After the weakening of the Nizam’s power, they gained greater control over the region. The Nizamat of Arcot included areas such as Arcot, Carnatic, and parts of modern-day Tamil Nadu.

Under the Vijayanagar Empire, there was a small feudal kingdom called Mysore. The Kingdom of Mysore was founded by the Wodeyar dynasty in 1399. The Wodeyar dynasty declared their independence following the fall of Vijayanagar in 1565, and Raja Wodeyar ascended the throne in 1578. The capital moved from Mysore to Srirangapatna in 1610.

subject: Medieval History
stem: According to Portuguese writer Nuniz, the women in the Vijayanagara Empire were experts in which of the following areas? 1. Wrestling 2. Astrology 3. Accounting 4. Soothsaying Select the correct answer using the code given below.

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: 1, 2, 3 and 4
solution.detail: The accounts of foreign travelers such as Abdur Razzak from Persia, Nicolo de Conte from Italy, Fernao Nuniz, Domingo Paes and Duarte Barbosa from Portugal, provide us with a valuable historical lens of the Vijayanagara Kingdom. Fernao Nuniz, a Portuguese traveler who visited the Vijayanagara Empire during the reign of Achyuta Deva Raya, noted that women in the empire held prominent roles. He observed that they were trained in various skills, including wrestling, swordsmanship, soothsaying, astrology, and accounting. These women were actively involved in the kingdom’s political, social, and cultural life. In addition to fine arts like music, some also received education in literature.

subject: Modern History
stem: With reference to 8th August 1942 in Indian history, which one of the following statements is correct?
options: The Quit India Resolution was adopted by the AICC.
The Viceroy’s Executive Council was expanded to include more Indians.
The Congress ministries resigned in seven provinces.
Cripps proposed an Indian Union with full Dominion Status once the Second World War was over.

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: The Quit India Resolution was adopted by the AICC.
solution.detail:
Option (a) is correct: The Quit India Resolution was passed by the All India Congress Committee (AICC) on 8th August 1942 at Gowalia Tank Maidan (now August Kranti Maidan) in Bombay, marking the launch of the Quit India Movement against British rule. Key Features of the Resolution:
- Demanded immediate end to British rule in India.
- Proposed the formation of a provisional government after British withdrawal.
- Declared that India would not support Britain in World War II unless granted independence.
- Gandhi’s famous call: "Do or Die" encouraged mass civil disobedience.

subject: Modern History
stem: In the context of Colonial India, Shah Nawaz Khan, Prem Kumar Sehgal and Gurbaksh Singh Dhillon are remembered as:
options: leaders of Swadeshi and Boycott Movement
members of the Interim Government in 1946
members of the Drafting Committee in the Constituent Assembly
officers of the Indian National Army

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: officers of the Indian National Army
solution.detail: In November 1945 officers of the Indian National Army (INA) were court-martialed at the Red Fort in Delhi. Among the senior officers tried were Major General Shah Nawaz Khan, Colonel Prem Kumar Sahgal, and Colonel Gurbaksh Singh Dhillon, trusted allies of Subhas Chandra Bose. Amid widespread protests and unrest across India, the British eventually released them. Jawaharlal Nehru, along with Bhulabhai Desai and Tej Bahadur Sapru, were part of the defence team of the INA officers in court.

stem: Consider the following statements: 1. The Montagu-Chelmsford Reforms of 1919 recommended granting voting rights to all the women above the age of 21. 2. The Government of India Act of 1935 gave women reserved seats in the legislature. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: 2 only
solution.detail: Statement (1) is incorrect: The Montagu-Chelmsford Reforms of 1919 introduced a diarchy system in provincial governments, where key areas like finance and law & order remained under British control, while other departments were transferred to Indian ministers. It also paved the way for the creation of a bicameral legislature at the centre. It recommended the voting rights to women in limited numbers to be extended on the basis of property, tax or education. Statement (2) is correct: Government of India Act, 1935 divided the powers between the Centre and provinces. It abolished dyarchy in the provinces and introduced ‘provincial autonomy’ in its place, while adopting the dyarchy at the Centre. It further extended the principle of communal representation to the depressed classes (Scheduled Castes), women and labour (workers).

subject: Modern History
stem: Who among the following was associated as Secretary with Hindu Female School which later came to be known as Bethune Female School?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Ishwar Chandra Vidyasagar
solution.detail: Ishwar Chandra Vidyasagar (1820-1891) was appointed as the secretary of the governing body of Bethune School/ College. The Bethune Female School, established in 1849 in Kolkata (then Calcutta) was one of the first schools in India dedicated to the education of girls. It was founded by John Elliot Drinkwater Bethune, a prominent social reformer and advocate for women's education in India. Vidyasagar was a strong advocate for women's rights and education, and his involvement with the Bethune School was part of his broader efforts to reform Indian society, including his campaigns for widow remarriage and the abolition of child marriage. It was mainly due to his efforts that the Hindu Widows’ Remarriage Act, 1856 was passed.

Blavatsky and Colonel Olcott in New York in 1875. In 1882, the headquarters of the Society were established in Adyar, near Madras (now Chennai) in India.

subject: Modern History
stem: Who among the following is associated with ‘Song from Prison’, a translation of ancient Indian religious lyrics in English?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Mohandas Karamchand Gandhi
solution.detail: “Songs from Prison" is a collection of ancient Indian religious lyrics translated into English by Mohandas Karamchand Gandhi. During his imprisonment in Yeravda Jail, Poona, in 1934, Gandhi undertook the task of translating these spiritual texts to make them accessible to a broader audience. His work reflects a deep engagement with spirituality and an effort to share the essence of Indian religious thought with the Englishspeaking world.
